title: acinos vulgaris var. villosus
intro: Acinos vulgaris var. villosus, also known as Woolly Chammis, is a perennial herb in the mint family, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of Acinos vulgaris var. villosus include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and promote a good night's sleep. Herbal preparations of Acinos vulgaris var. villosus can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
